candidateHome.title: Your Assessment Journey
candidateHome.intro: A structured experience designed to help you demonstrate your strengths. Follow each step at your own pace.
candidateHome.mapSubtitle: Follow the steps below to complete your assessment center experience.

adminDashboard.title: Assessment Center Command
adminDashboard.intro: Track candidate, assessor, and report progress across every stage of every engagement - from brief to released report.
adminDashboard.mapSubtitle: Continuous assessment cycle - track progress across all stages.

adminClients.subtitle: Manage client organizations and contacts.

report.title: Assessment Report
report.notAvailable: Your report is not yet available.

candidateSkills.title: My Skills
candidateSkills.rolePlaceholder: Hello {{name}} - your skill map will appear here once a role profile has been assigned to you.
candidateSkills.noProfileBody: An administrator hasn't linked you to a role profile yet, so there are no target proficiencies to compare your scores against. Check back after the assessment has been set up, or contact your VIFM facilitator if you think this is a mistake.
candidateSkills.migrationMissing: Admin: run npx supabase db push to apply migration 00016 before binding candidates to role profiles.
candidateSkills.noCompetencies: This role profile has no competencies linked yet. Ask your administrator to populate the profile.

stats.subtitle: Based on consensus ratings from your assessment
stats.progressBlurb: How much of your role profile has been assessed.
stats.byDomainBlurb: How your role profile is distributed across the VIFM framework.
stats.avgByDomainBlurb: Your average BARS score (1–5) per VIFM domain. 0 = not yet assessed.

quiz.endSessionConfirm: End this session? Your unanswered questions will count as zero. You can retake at any time.
quiz.autoSavedHint: Your answer for this question is saved automatically.

results.headerWellDoneBody: Great work on this competency. Try a harder one next.
results.headerKeepLearningBody: You can retake the quiz at any time to improve your score.
results.headerSessionEndedBody: You can retake the quiz at any time to improve your score.

notifications.candidateEmpty: You're all caught up - no notifications yet.

candidateWelcome.intro: Welcome to the VIFM Assessment Center. You have been selected to participate in an assessment for the role outlined below. This process is designed to evaluate your competencies through a series of structured exercises.

form.title: Your Profile Details
form.subtitle: Please provide the following information for tracking and identification purposes.

candidateConsent.title: Consent & Data Protection
candidateConsent.noticeIntro: In accordance with the UAE Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021 on Personal Data Protection, the Saudi Arabia Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL), and the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), we are required to obtain your explicit consent before collecting and processing your personal data.
candidateConsent.noticeCollect: Virginia Institute of Finance and Management (VIFM) will collect and process the following data during the assessment:
candidateConsent.collectItem4: Audio/video recordings (if applicable for virtual sessions)
candidateConsent.noticeRetention: Your data will be retained for a maximum of 2 years from the assessment date unless otherwise agreed contractually. You have the right to access, correct, or request deletion of your data at any time.
candidateConsent.readConfirm: I confirm I have read and understood the notice above.
candidateConsent.dataConsent: I consent to the collection and processing of my personal data as described above for the purpose of the assessment center evaluation. I understand my rights under applicable data protection laws.
candidateConsent.assessmentConsent: I voluntarily agree to participate in the assessment center and understand that the results will be shared with the sponsoring organization for the purpose of talent evaluation and development.
candidateConsent.contactConsent: I agree that VIFM can contact me, including by email, in order to participate in future test trials, surveys, and to provide further information relating to the assessment.
candidateConsent.clientForms: I agree to complete any additional forms provided by the sponsoring organization before the start of the assessment (e.g., application forms, surveys).

candidateAssessments.title: Your Assessments
candidateAssessments.noExercises: No exercises scheduled yet. Check back closer to your assessment date.
candidateAssessments.important1: Each exercise is designed to assess specific competencies relevant to the target role. Please arrive prepared and on time for each session.
candidateAssessments.important2: During the exercises, trained assessors will observe your behavior and record observations. There are no right or wrong answers - the assessment evaluates how you approach situations naturally.
candidateAssessments.important3: Your results will be compiled into a confidential assessment report, which will be shared with you once finalized.

candidateReport.title: Your Assessment Report
candidateReport.notAvailableTitle: Your report is not yet available.
candidateReport.notAvailableBody: Assessment results are currently being processed. You will be notified when your report is ready for viewing.
candidateReport.fullReportBody: Detailed behavioral evidence, scores, and competency findings (~6 pages).
candidateReport.learningPlanBody: Your 30 / 60 / 90 day development roadmap with targeted actions per competency.

candidatePathway.title: My learning pathway
candidatePathway.subtitle: AI turns your assessed gaps into a sequenced, course-by-course development plan.

academy.recommendedBlurb: Chosen from your assessment to close your highest-priority skill gaps.
academy.notEnrolledTitle: You are not enrolled in any courses yet
academy.notEnrolledBody: Explore the VIFM catalogue to find a programme that fits your goals.

course.completeEveryLesson: Complete every lesson to earn your credential.
course.allLessonsDone: All lessons done - claim your credential.

lesson.noContent: This lesson has no detailed content yet. Complete the knowledge check below to progress.
lesson.reviewTopic: Review this topic, then complete the knowledge check below.
lesson.checkIntro: Take a short check to confirm you have absorbed this lesson. You need {{pct}}% to pass. You can retake it any time.
lesson.retakeTip: Retake the check to improve your score - your latest attempt counts.
lesson.startCheckFail: Could not start the knowledge check.
lesson.submitCheckFail: Could not submit the knowledge check.

enroll.enrollFail: Could not enroll. Please try again.

complete.issueFail: Could not issue your credential. Please try again.

credentials.title: My Credentials
credentials.subtitle: Verifiable credentials issued by VIFM. Share the verification link or download the certificate.
credentials.empty: You have no credentials yet. Complete a course or assessment to earn one.

adminAssessors.title: Assessors
adminAssessors.subtitle: Manage assessor pool, certifications, and assignments.
adminAssessors.emptyBody: Assessors are created when assigning them to engagements.

adminSettings.title: Settings
adminSettings.subtitle: System configuration, integrations, and compliance settings.

auth.name: Supabase Auth
auth.description: Authentication and role-based access control - not yet enabled

ai.name: AI Features (Anthropic Claude)
ai.descriptionOn: AI observation classifier, report writer, development recommender, bias detector
ai.descriptionOff: Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in environment to enable AI features

email.name: Email (SendGrid / Resend)
email.descriptionOff: Set EMAIL_PROVIDER and EMAIL_API_KEY to enable email notifications

video.name: Video (Daily.co)
video.descriptionOff: Set DAILY_API_KEY to enable video conferencing

i18n.name: i18n / Arabic RTL
i18n.description: English and Arabic translations with RTL support

compliance.uaeLaw: UAE Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021 (Data Protection)
compliance.pdpl: Saudi Arabia Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL)
compliance.acGuidelines: International Taskforce on Assessment Center Guidelines (6th Edition)
compliance.retention: Data retention: maximum 2 years unless contractually extended

adminAnalytics.title: Analytics
adminAnalytics.subtitle: System-wide analytics: ICC scores, bias detection, engagement metrics.
adminAnalytics.candidateComparisonSubtitle: Compare candidates by their Overall Assessment Rating.
adminAnalytics.benchmarksEmpty: Benchmark data will populate as more assessments are completed.
adminAnalytics.benchmarksNormGroups: Norm groups: GCC Banking, MENA Corporate, Global Corporate
adminAnalytics.biasSubtitle: Identifies potential rating biases across assessors. Values outside normal ranges are flagged.

list.title: Projects
list.subtitle: Create and manage assessment center projects.

detail.coursesDescCandidate: Per-candidate - courses ranked by this candidate's competency gaps × course relevance. Use this view to anchor a 1:1 development conversation.
detail.coursesDescCohort: Cohort-aggregated - courses ranked by total gap severity × course relevance across every candidate in the engagement. Higher fit score = larger development impact across the group.
detail.coursesEmptyCandidate: No course recommendations for {{name}} - either no consensus ratings have been finalised, this candidate is on or above target across all competencies, or the catalogue doesn't yet cover the relevant competencies.
detail.coursesEmptyCohort: No course recommendations yet - either no consensus ratings have been finalised, the cohort is on or above target across all competencies, or the catalogue doesn't yet cover the relevant competencies.
detail.confirmRemoveCandidate: Remove candidate "{{name}}"? This will also delete their assignments.
detail.priorLinkTitle: View the prior engagement this one was seeded from
detail.reengageDialogBody: Creates a new draft engagement seeded from this one. The role profile, competencies, exercises, and matrix carry over so you don't have to rebuild the design. Prior assessor assignments, ratings, and reports are not copied - the new run earns its own scores.
detail.reengageCarryCandidates: Carry over the candidates from this engagement (each new candidate row is linked to its prior so deltas can show on the report).
detail.roleProfileHelp: Defines target proficiencies the candidate's scores will be compared against.
detail.viewAsCandidate: View as candidate (opens in new tab)
detail.noAssignments: No assignments yet. Add candidates and assessors, then create assignments.
detail.integrationDesc: Consolidated view of all assessors' preliminary ratings and notes.
detail.noIntegration: No integration worksheets submitted yet. Assessors complete these before the wash-up session.
detail.candidateReportsDesc: Select report types and generate or share reports with candidates.
detail.confirmStatusBody: Are you sure you want to {{action}} this engagement? This action changes the engagement status to

talentMap.title: Talent Map
talentMap.nineBoxDesc: Performance = RESULTS + PEOPLE scores. Potential = THINKING + SELF scores. Both on the 1-5 consensus scale.
talentMap.nineBoxEmpty: No consensus scores recorded yet - the grid populates once wash-up ratings are finalised.
talentMap.successionDescRole: Pipeline for the {{role}} role, by overall assessment recommendation.
talentMap.successionDescGeneric: Pipeline for the target role, by overall assessment recommendation.
talentMap.heatmapDesc: Consensus competency scores across the cohort. Greener is stronger; redder needs development.
talentMap.heatmapEmpty: No competency scores to chart yet.

page.title: New Engagement
page.subtitle: Create a new assessment center engagement in 5 steps.

step1.title: Step 1: Basic Information
step1.name: Name *
step1.projectNamePlaceholder: e.g., ADNOC Senior Manager AC - April 2026
step1.endDateError: End date must be after start date

step3.title: Step 3: Select Exercises
step3.name: Name *
step3.emptyLibrary: No exercises in the library yet. Create one using the button above.

step4.title: Step 4: Exercise-Competency Matrix
step4.emptyState: Select at least 1 exercise and 4 competencies in the previous steps.
step4.guideline: Each competency must be observed in at least 2 exercises (International Taskforce Guidelines).

jd.dialogDescription: Paste a job description (English or Arabic). Claude maps it to VIFM's 38-competency framework with suggested weights and priorities. You can then refine before applying.
jd.pastePlaceholder: Paste the full job description here. Both English and Arabic are supported.
jd.uploadPrompt: Click to upload a JD file
jd.uploadHint: PDF or TXT · max 10MB · English or Arabic
jd.analyzingBody: Claude is mapping the JD to VIFM's competency framework. This usually takes 15–30 seconds.
jd.pickAtLeastOneToast: Pick at least one competency to apply.
jd.maxExceededToast: Step 2 allows max 15 competencies; you've picked {{count}}.

roleProfile.dialogDescription: Pick a pre-built competency pack. Applying a profile replaces your current Step 2 selection. You can refine afterwards.
roleProfile.noCompetenciesToast: This role profile has no competencies yet.
roleProfile.tooManyToast: Profile has {{count}} competencies; Step 2 allows max 15.

list.title: Role Profiles
list.subtitle: Reusable competency packs tied to common roles. Pick a profile when creating a new engagement to pre-fill Step 2 with the right competencies, weights, and priorities.
list.emptyBody: The seed migration ships 6 GCC banking + government profiles. Run the migrations or create one from a job description.

new.title: New Role Profile
new.subtitle: Create a reusable competency pack. Use "Build from JD" to let Claude pre-populate competencies from a job description, or add them manually.

delete.dialogDescription: This deletes the "{{name}}" profile and all its competency mappings. Engagements that previously copied from this profile keep their competencies — only the template is removed.

editor.errMinComps: Pick at least 4 competencies before saving.
editor.targetProficiencyHint: BARS level expected for the role (1–5). Drives gap badges in reports.
editor.descriptionPlaceholder: Short description of the role and what makes it distinctive.
editor.competenciesEmpty: No competencies yet. Use "Build from JD" for an AI suggestion or add them manually.
editor.reasoningPlaceholder: One sentence on why this competency matters here.

bulkAssign.title: Bulk-Link Candidates to Role Profiles
bulkAssign.subtitleAfter: columns to assign existing candidates to role profiles in one step. Matching is by email; candidates without a matching email are skipped (and flagged in the result).
bulkAssign.warnNoMatch: Line {{line}}: profile "{{raw}}" did not match any role profile by id or name.
bulkAssign.errNoRows: No valid rows found in the CSV.
bulkAssign.errValidation: Validation failed. Check the CSV format.
bulkAssign.pasteHint: Or paste rows directly. Header optional.
bulkAssign.defaultProfileLabel: Default role profile (used when CSV column is blank)
bulkAssign.defaultProfilePlaceholder: None — clear assignment for blank rows

bulkImport.title: Bulk JD Import
bulkImport.subtitle: Upload up to 25 job descriptions (PDF or TXT) at once. Claude extracts a recommended competency set per file; you review names + accept which to save as new role profiles.
bulkImport.errPickFiles: Pick one or more PDF/TXT files first.
bulkImport.errPickRow: Pick at least one row with a non-empty name.
bulkImport.extractingNote: Each file takes ~15–30s. The whole batch is one server call.
bulkImport.namesNote: Names default to the file name; edit before saving.

list.title: Training Courses
list.subtitle: VIFM's course catalogue. Each course maps to Assessment Center behavioural competencies and ARA pillars so the recommender can surface the right development path for each diagnostic.
list.migrationAfter: into the SQL editor) to create the catalogue tables. The page will populate once the migration is in place.
list.glanceEmptyMid: to ingest course outlines in bulk via AI extraction, or

new.title: New course
new.descPre: Add a course manually. To bulk-load 80 PDFs in one shot, use
new.descPost: on the courses list - that route runs AI extraction across the six standard blocks.

edit.desc: Edit the catalogue entry. The mapping panel below shows the Assessment Center competency + ARA pillar tags that drive the recommender - edit them when you spot an over- or under-tag from the AI extraction.

import.title: Import course PDFs
import.desc: Drop one or many course outline PDFs (English or Arabic, 6-block format). Claude reads the six blocks, populates the structured catalogue fields, and proposes Assessment Center competency + ARA pillar tags with a 1-sentence rationale per tag. You review per-row and accept / skip before any catalogue rows are written.
import.maxBatch: Up to 25 PDFs per batch - split larger uploads
import.extractedWithMatches: Extracted {{ok}} of {{total}} PDFs · {{matched}} match existing course(s)
import.dropHint: Drop PDFs here or click to browse
import.dropSubhint: Up to 25 PDFs per batch · processed 5 in parallel · ~10-30s per file
import.existingFound: Existing course found - uncheck to import as new

duplicates.title: Duplicate-finder
duplicates.subtitle: Near-match courses by Levenshtein similarity on title (whitespace + case-insensitive). Pairs at or above {{pct}}% similarity are listed, ranked by confidence. Use the X button on the row you want to drop - the other one stays.
duplicates.migrationMissing: Migration 00023 not yet applied - run it to enable the catalogue tables.
duplicates.emptyPre: No courses in the catalogue yet - nothing to compare. Use
duplicates.emptyPost: to populate, then come back here.
duplicates.noPairs: No near-match pairs found. The catalogue is clean of title duplicates above the {{pct}}% threshold.

quotes.title: Quote requests
quotes.subtitlePre: Inbound leads from the public training catalogue at
quotes.emptyFiltered: No requests in the "{{status}}" pipeline today.
quotes.emptyAll: No quote requests yet. As prospects submit forms from /courses they'll appear here.

quoteDetail.programmeDetailsDesc: What the requester captured at submission. Use this when scoping the quote.
quoteDetail.internalNotesDesc: Sales notes for VIFM use only. Not visible to the requester.

quoteActions.statusHint: Moving to contacted, quoted, won or lost stamps the corresponding timeline timestamp.
quoteActions.notesPlaceholder: e.g. Phoned 2026-04-30 - wants Q3 pricing, group of 18, hybrid delivery, in EN.

form.block1Placeholder: Block 1 - paste the course overview paragraph.
form.block2Placeholder:
Block 2 - one competency phrase per line. e.g.
Detection Capabilities
Incident Response
Digital Forensics
Risk Mitigation
form.block2Help: These are the PDF's own topical "Target Competencies" list (e.g., "Bookkeeping Automation"), kept verbatim for audit. The mapping to the Assessment Center's 38 behavioural competencies and ARA's 8 pillars is a separate step (Day 3).
form.block3Placeholder:
Block 3 - one objective per line. e.g.
Apply prompt engineering techniques
Create content using AI tools
Analyse business data with AI
form.block5Placeholder: Block 5 - how the course is delivered (lectures, case studies, hands-on, etc.).
form.block6PlaceholderL1: # MAIN HEADER (use # at the start of the line)
form.block6PlaceholderL2: ## Sub-header (use ## - optional, only when the section has named sub-headers)
form.block6PlaceholderL6: - Bullet without any sub-header above it works too
form.block6HelpE: is a sub-bullet. Each section uses either flat bullets
form.block6HelpF: sub-headers, never both. Saved structure round-trips back into this textarea on edit.
form.block7Placeholder: Internal note - not from the PDF, not shown to clients. e.g. 'Run only with senior cohort', 'Pricing reviewed Q1 2026'.
form.block7Help: Free-text admin annotation. Not part of the source PDF, not surfaced on client-facing reports. Shown only here and on the recommender's internal panel.
form.blockOrderFootnote: Six-block order: 1 Overview · 2 Target competencies · 3 Objectives · 4 Target audience · 5 Methodology · 6 Detailed outline - all editable here. Day 3 adds the Assessment Center competency / ARA pillar mapping panel and an Arabic-side editor.

tags.title: Recommender mappings
tags.descPre: Two-axis tagging that drives both Assessment Center and ARA recommendations. Hover the AI rationale text under each tag to see the extractor's reasoning. Edit weights or remove tags below; changes are local until you click
tags.noCompetencyTags: No competency tags yet. Add one below - courses without competency tags won't surface on Assessment Center engagement recommendations.
tags.noPillarTags: No pillar tags yet. Courses without pillar tags won't surface on ARA capability-building plans.
tags.rationalePlaceholder: Rationale (why does this course develop this competency / pillar?)
tags.weightFootnotePre: Weight {{weight}} ({{label}}) - used in the recommender as

delete.confirmBodyPost: will be removed from the catalogue, along with its Assessment Center competency and ARA pillar tag mappings. This does not affect any past assessments or candidate reports. This action cannot be undone.
